title: Support for modport expressions
body:
It would be nice to support modport expressions like this:

```systemverilog
interface my_if;

  logic start;        // Start the operation
  logic done;         // Operation done

  modport dut (input  .start_i (start),
               output .done_o  (done));

endinterface
```
They appear in section 25.5.4 of IEEE 1800-2017 (and probably earlier versions of the standard: I haven't checked). I know that there was a "should we support this? no" question/answer in issue #1861, but I've started a new issue to track it as a feature request.

One reason that this is nice is that it allows modules to take an interface, but still keep the "foo_i is an input; foo_o is an output" naming convention.

Presumably the "trivial rename" support used above isn't particularly difficult, and I think it would already be nice as a stepping-stone. The full syntax in the spec allows an (optional) expression inside the parens. The standard is a bit vague on this point, but I guess an input can be any valid non-empty expression and an output can be assigned to nothing or to any valid lvalue in the scope of the interface (the same as a port assignment for a module instantiation). I don't know how much more difficult this general expression support would be: I'd be interested to hear.
